section.content:
After agenda confirmation, iterate through each section:
```xml <step n="2.6" goal="Deep discovery for each agenda section" for-each="section in agenda_sections">
<!-- Sub-step A: Key Message Discovery --> <action>Generate 3-4 key message options for {{section.title}}: - Option 1: Direct/assertive framing - Option 2: Question-based framing - Option 3: Story-based framing - Option 4: Data-driven framing </action>
<action>Use AskUserQuestion tool to present message options (single-select)</action> <action>Store selection as {{section.key_message}}</action> ```

INIT → CONTEXT_COLLECTION → AGENDA_GENERATION → AGENDA_SELECTION → ↓ For each section (state = SECTION_DISCOVERY): MESSAGE_OPTIONS → CONTENT_SELECTION → VISUAL_TREATMENT → RESEARCH_OFFER → [RESEARCH_RESULTS] → SECTION_REFINEMENT → SECTION_COMPLETE ↓ → SLIDE_GENERATION → MODIFICATION_LOOP → SAVE
**Message Options (Phase 2a):** - 1 question per section - multiSelect: false - Options: 3-4 message framings

**Story 2 - Message Discovery:** - [ ] For each section, 3-4 message framings presented - [ ] Options include: direct, question, story, data framings - [ ] Single-select UI (not multi-select) for message choice - [ ] Selection stored in section.discovery.key_message

All workflows follow the BMAD pattern:
1. **Load** `workflow.yaml` from the workflow directory 2. **Read** `instructions.md` for step-by-step execution 3. **Execute** steps in numerical order 4. **Pause** at `<template-output>` and `<ask>` tags for user interaction 5. **Update** `.slide-builder/status.yaml` with progress

section.notes: - Step 2.5 generates and confirms agenda_sections array - Each section has: id, title, narrative_role, estimated_slides, description - Uses AskUserQuestion with multiSelect: true for section selection - Custom sections handled via "Other" option parsing - Outputs "Proceeding to generate detailed slide breakdown..." at end - INSERT Step 2.6 AFTER line 309 (after the closing output tag)
section.key-code:
<!-- From lines 287-308 --> <action>Store confirmed sections as {{agenda_sections}} array with full structure: - Each section includes: id, title, narrative_role, estimated_slides, description - Preserve selection order as presentation order - This variable is available for Phase 2 discovery (Story 13.2) </action>
<output> **Agenda Confirmed**
Your presentation will follow this structure:
{{for each section in agenda_sections}} {{loop_index}}. **{{section.title}}** ({{section.narrative_role}}) - ~{{section.estimated_slides}} slide(s) {{end for}}
Total estimated slides: {{sum of estimated_slides}}
Proceeding to generate detailed slide breakdown...
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ </output> </step>

section.topic: Step 3 - Slide Generation (Comes AFTER new Step 2.6)
section.notes: - Step 3 generates individual slides from agenda structure - Uses catalog.json for template matching - Storyline structure generated from collected context - This step should use discovery.key_message from Step 2.6

section.key-code: # Generated - Step 2.5 (Epic 13: Enhanced Planning) proposed_agenda: [] # Generated agenda sections before user selection agenda_sections: [] # User-confirmed agenda sections for Phase 2 discovery
section.notes: - agenda_sections already exists in workflow variables - Discovery data stored IN each section object (section.discovery.key_message) - No new top-level variables needed for Story 13.2

interface.name: agenda_sections Array Structure
interface.description: Input structure from Story 13.1, to be enhanced with discovery data

interface.name: AskUserQuestion Tool
interface.description: Claude Code's structured question tool for single-select message framings

interface.name: Narrative Role to Framing Style Mapping
interface.description: Map section narrative_role to appropriate message framing styles
interface.mapping:
narrative_role → framing_styles:
opening: - direct: "{{provocative_statement_about_audience_situation}}" - question: "What if {{desirable_outcome}}?" - story: "{{time_reference}}, {{relatable_scenario}}..." - data: "{{surprising_statistic}} of {{audience_group}} {{situation}}"
context: - direct: "The landscape has shifted..." - question: "What's driving this change?" - story: "Here's where we are today..." - data: "{{market_data}} shows {{trend}}"
problem: - direct: "{{pain_point}} is costing you {{consequence}}" - question: "Why do {{audience_group}} struggle with {{challenge}}?" - story: "Meet {{persona}}, who faces {{challenge}} every day" - data: "{{statistic}} shows the impact of {{problem}}"
solution: - direct: "{{solution}} delivers {{key_benefit}}" - question: "What would {{desirable_outcome}} mean for your team?" - story: "Here's how {{customer}} achieved {{result}}" - data: "{{solution}} reduces {{metric}} by {{percentage}}"
evidence: - direct: "The results speak for themselves" - question: "How do we know this works?" - story: "{{customer_name}}'s transformation" - data: "{{metric_improvement}} across {{number}} implementations"
cta: - direct: "Let's start with {{specific_next_step}}" - question: "Ready to {{action}}?" - story: "Join {{number}} teams who have already {{action}}" - data: "{{time_to_value}} to see first results"

constraints.constraint: AskUserQuestion has maximum 4 options per question. Story 13.2 requires exactly 3-4 message framings per section, which fits within this limit.
Message framing selection MUST use multiSelect: false (single-select). This is explicitly required by AC #3.
Step 2.6 uses for-each pattern: `for-each="section in agenda_sections"` Each section is processed sequentially, not in parallel.
Key message stored in `section.discovery.key_message`, NOT a separate array. The discovery object is nested within each section object.
When user selects "Other" and provides custom text, store that exact text as key_message. Do not attempt to categorize or transform custom input.

Message framings must be contextual to: 1. section.title - The specific section being processed 2. section.narrative_role - Determines framing style (opening vs problem vs cta etc) 3. section.description - Additional context for relevance

dependency.name: Story 13.1: Agenda Structure Generation
dependency.provides: - {{agenda_sections}} variable with confirmed sections - Each section has: id, title, narrative_role, estimated_slides, description - Step 2.5 implementation pattern and XML structure

dependency.name: AskUserQuestion
dependency.documentation: Built-in Claude Code tool for structured user input

dependency.name: .slide-builder/workflows/plan-deck/instructions.md
dependency.purpose: Target file for Step 2.6 insertion (after line 309)

test-scenario.name: Happy Path - All Sections
test-scenario.steps: 1. Run `/sb:plan-deck` 2. Complete steps 1-2.5 (context collection, agenda confirmation) 3. For first section, verify 3-4 message framings appear 4. Select a framing (not "Other") 5. Repeat for all remaining sections 6. Verify all sections have discovery.key_message populated
test-scenario.expected: Each section's AskUserQuestion shows contextual framings based on narrative_role

test-scenario.name: Custom Message via Other
test-scenario.steps: 1. Run `/sb:plan-deck` through step 2.5 2. When message options appear, select "Other" 3. Enter custom message text 4. Proceed to next section 5. Verify custom text stored in discovery.key_message
test-scenario.expected: Custom text preserved exactly as entered

test-scenario.name: Single-Select Enforcement
test-scenario.steps: 1. Run workflow to step 2.6 2. Observe the AskUserQuestion UI 3. Verify only one option can be selected (radio buttons, not checkboxes)
test-scenario.expected: UI shows single-select mode, not multi-select

test-scenario.name: Narrative Role Contextuality
test-scenario.steps: 1. Run workflow with mixed narrative_role sections (opening, problem, solution, cta) 2. For each section, observe the message framing options 3. Verify framings match the narrative_role guidance
test-scenario.expected: - Opening sections → Hook-style framings - Problem sections → Pain-point framings - Solution sections → Value-proposition framings - CTA sections → Action-oriented framings

verification-checklist.item: Message framing options appear for each agenda section
Options are contextual to the section's narrative role
No console errors during workflow execution
All sections have discovery.key_message after completion

implementation-notes.note: Insert Step 2.6 AFTER the closing `` tag of Step 2.5 (line 309). The new step comes BEFORE Step 3 (narrative structure generation).
Follow the same XML pattern established in Step 2.5: - Use `` - Use `` for instructions - Use `` for formatted messages showing progress - Use section markers with `` for readability
Display which section is being processed: ``` **Section Discovery: {{section.title}}** ({{loop_index}} of {{total_sections}}) ```
No explicit error handling needed - AskUserQuestion tool handles empty responses and provides "Other" option automatically.

This story only adds key_message discovery. Stories 13.3 and 13.4 will add: - section.discovery.content_types (13.3) - section.discovery.visual_treatment (13.3) - Integration with slide generation schema (13.4)
